text: Has the news of the Overwhelming Event reached you ňĻO Prophetňļ?
On that Day ňĻsomeňļ faces will be downcast,
ňĻtotallyňļ overburdened, exhausted,
burning in a scorching Fire,
left to drink from a scalding spring.
They will have no food except a foul, thorny shrub,
neither nourishing nor satisfying hunger.
On that Day ňĻotherňļ faces will be glowing with bliss,
ňĻfullyňļ pleased with their striving,
in an elevated Garden,
where no idle talk will be heard.
In it will be a running spring,
along with thrones raised high,
and cups set at hand,
and ňĻfineňļ cushions lined up,
and ňĻsplendidňļ carpets spread out.
Do they not ever reflect on camels‚ÄĒhow they were ňĻmasterfullyňļ created; <sup foot_note=1302>1302</sup>

foot_notes.1302: Another possible translation: ‚ÄúDo they not ever reflect on rainclouds‚ÄĒhow they are formed?‚ÄĚ

text: and the sky‚ÄĒhow it was raised ňĻhighňļ;
and the mountains‚ÄĒhow they were firmly set up;
and the earth‚ÄĒhow it was levelled out? <sup foot_note=1303>1303</sup>

foot_notes.1303: With the exception of some mountains and hills in some parts of the world, the earth has been smoothed out, making it easy for people to live, farm, and travel.

text: So, ňĻcontinue toňļ remind ňĻall, O Prophetňļ, for your duty is only to remind.
You are not ňĻthereňļ to compel them ňĻto believeňļ.
But whoever turns away, persisting in disbelief,
then Allah will inflict upon them the major punishment. <sup foot_note=1304>1304</sup>

foot_notes.1304: The Hellfire is the major punishment. The minor punishment includes disasters in this life as well as torments in the grave. See 32:21.

text: Surely to Us is their return,
then surely with Us is their reckoning.
